sections.content:
The magenta square moved from [32-33, 38-39] to [27-28, 32-33], which is inside the blue blob. The blob's pixel count went from 69 to 65, a decrease of 4 pixels (the size of the magenta square).
So the magenta square moved into the blob, and the blob lost 4 pixels. This is the opposite of what happened with sky blue dots (blob gained pixels when dots were absorbed).
Now I have 3 magenta squares remaining outside the blob:
- [40-41, 19-20]
- [54-55, 47-48]
- [55-56, 14-15]
And 1 magenta square inside the blob at [27-28, 32-33].

The blob is at [23-31, 29-37]. I need to move the magenta squares to positions inside the blob.
Let me move the next magenta square. The one at [40-41, 19-20] is closest to the blob. Let me click at a position inside the blob, say [28, 33] again.
But wait, [28, 33] is now occupied by the magenta square I just moved there. Let me click at a different position inside the blob, say [26, 34].
Actually, let me think about this more carefully. The magenta square moved from [32-33, 38-39] to [27-28, 32-33] when I clicked at [28, 33]. So the square moved to the clicked position (or nearby).
To move the magenta square at [40-41, 19-20] into the blob, I need to click at a position inside the blob that is closer to [40-41, 19-20] than to any other magenta square.
Let me check distances:

- [55-56, 14-15] center is approximately [55.5, 14.5]
- [27-28, 32-33] center is approximately [27.5, 32.5] (inside blob)
If I click at [26, 34], the distances are:
- From [40.5, 19.5] to [26, 34]: 14.5 + 14.5 = 29
- From [54.5, 47.5] to [26, 34]: 28.5 + 13.5 = 42
- From [55.5, 14.5] to [26, 34]: 29.5 + 19.5 = 49
- From [27.5, 32.5] to [26, 34]: 1.5 + 1.5 = 3
So the nearest magenta square to [26, 34] is the one already inside the blob. This would move the wrong square.
I need to click at a position that is closer to [40-41, 19-20] than to any other square.
Let me try clicking at [35, 25], which is closer to [40-41, 19-20].
Distance from [40.5, 19.5] to [35, 25]: 5.5 + 5.5 = 11
Distance from [27.5, 32.5] to [35, 25]: 7.5 + 7.5 = 15
So [35, 25] is closer to [40-41, 19-20]. But is [35, 25] inside the blob? The blob is at [23-31, 29-37], so [35, 25] is outside the blob.
Hmm, I need to click at a position inside the blob that is closer to [40-41, 19-20] than to any other square.
The blob is at [23-31, 29-37]. The closest point in the blob to [40-41, 19-20] is [31, 29].
Distance from [40.5, 19.5] to [31, 29]: 9.5 + 9.5 = 19
Distance from [27.5, 32.5] to [31, 29]: 3.5 + 3.5 = 7
So [31, 29] is closer to the square already inside the blob. This won't work.
Maybe I need to move the magenta squares one at a time, and the blob can hold multiple squares. Let me try clicking at [31, 29] to see what happens.

Let me try clicking at [31, 29] to move the magenta square at [40-41, 19-20] into the blob.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move magenta square [40-41, 19-20] into blob by clicking at [31, 29]
predict(expect_change=True, note='Moving magenta square into blob')
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 31, 'col': 29}])
print("Result:", last_action_result)
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Magenta nodes after:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'M':
        print(f"  id={n['id']} boundary={n['boundary']}")
print("Blue blob:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'b':
        print(f"  id={n['id']} pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
